questions.prompt: Some students choose a major before beginning college, while others need time to explore different fields. Some people believe colleges should require students to attend career exploration workshops before selecting a major so they can learn about job options, salaries, required skills, and transfer pathways. Others believe students should be free to choose a major without additional requirements because they already receive advice from family, teachers, or personal research. Assignment: Write an essay of 300–600 words in which you develop your point of view on the issue presented in the passage. Support your position with reasoning and examples from your reading, studies, experience, or observations.
questions.modelAnswer: A strong response should state a clear position on requiring career exploration workshops before students select a major, support that position with specific reasons and examples, address the issue thoughtfully, and demonstrate organized, controlled writing.
questions.scoringGuidance: A strong response should state a clear position on requiring career exploration workshops before students select a major, support that position with specific reasons and examples, address the issue thoughtfully, and demonstrate organized, controlled writing.
questions.rubric: Scores range from 8 for a focused, well-developed, logically organized essay with strong language control to 0 for a blank, off-topic, too short, incoherent, or non-English response.
